sentence: HPI: Randy started feeling unwell around supper time last night with a runny nose, which is currently clear, and a cough that is not barky.
He has been pulling on his ears and rubbing his nose, and has a low-grade fever of 100.3 degrees rectal temperature.
His appetite has been variable, with some eating and some not eating.
He has been irritable and having trouble sleeping.
He has been exposed to his older sister, who has been sick, and a nephew who had a sinus infection.
The patient's mother has been using saline mist, Tylenol, baby Vicks, and a humidifier.
Tylenol (dose not specified) as needed for fever
Saline mist as needed for congestion
Baby Vicks as needed for congestion
Assessment: Randy has a viral upper respiratory tract infection with a low-grade fever and non-barky cough.
Problem list: Upper respiratory tract infection; viral infection; fever; cough; cradle cap
I prescribed Zarbee's for his stuffy nose and ordered a medication for his cough, to be given only if he develops a barky cough.
The medication will be given in a half a teaspoon dose for five days.
I also recommended the use of head & shoulders, selsun blue, or nizoral ad shampoo for his cradle cap, to be used two times per week, and to use a soft brush and baby oil to remove scales.
Follow-up: Randy will be seen in one week, and both he and his sister will be seen in the same week.
